#  How do you add bank accounts to Procore Pay? 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
### Background
To learn about the bank accounts needed to send and receive payments with Procore Pay, please read [What are the different types of bank accounts in Procore Pay?](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/about-bank-accounts)
### Answer
How and when you add a bank account to Procore Pay depends on your role in the payment process:
#### To send payments with Procore Pay
To send payments with Procore Pay, a payor must have at least two (2) bank accounts:
  * **Deposit Account**. One of the first steps to complete during the Procore Pay implementation process is to open at least one (1) deposit account. This requires the assistance of the [Payments Operations](https://v2.support.procore.com/reference-procore-pay-support) team at Procore. Your company can also request to open additional deposit accounts later. See [Open a Deposit Account for Procore Pay as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/open-deposit-accounts).
  * **Funding Account**. After opening a deposit account, a Payments Admin can add one of your company's bank accounts to the Company level Payments Tool. You must have an open deposit account before you can complete the steps in [Add a Funding Account](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/add-funding-accounts).


#### To receive payments with Procore Pay
To receive payments from a General Contractor using Procore Pay, a payee must have at least one (1) bank account. To learn how to add it to Procore Pay, see [Add a Bank Account as a Payee](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payee-setup-guide/add-your-bank-account).
## See Also

  * [How do funds flow between bank accounts with Procore Pay?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-do-funds-flow-between-bank-accounts-with-procore-pay)
  * [What are the different types of bank accounts in Procore Pay?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-the-different-types-of-bank-accounts-in-procore-pay)
title: How do you add bank accounts to Procore Pay?
description: Describes the different types of bank accounts in Procore Pay.
